2605.17352.title: AMATA: Adaptive Multi-Agent Trajectory Alignment for Knowledge-Intensive Question Answering
2605.17352.paper_summary: Despite substantial advances in large language models (LLMs), generating factually consistent responses for knowledge-intensive question answering remains challenging. These difficulties are primarily due to hallucinations and the limitations of LLMs in bridging long-tail knowledge gaps. To address this, we propose AMATA, an Adaptive Multi-Agent Trajectory Alignment framework that dynamically integrates external knowledge to improve response interpretability and factual grounding. Our architecture leverages six specialized agents that collaboratively perform structured actions for complex question reasoning. We formalize multi-agent collaboration with external tools as a trajectory preference alignment problem, incorporating question-aware agent customization and inter-agent preference harmonization. AMATA introduces two principal innovations: (1) Intra-Trajectory Preference Learning, which learns objective-oriented preferences to prioritize critical agents, and (2) Inter-Agent Dependency Learning, which captures cross-agent tool dependencies through a novel dependency-aware direct preference optimization technique. Empirical results show that AMATA consistently outperforms baseline approaches, knowledge-augmented frameworks, and LLM-based trajectory systems on five established knowledge-intensive QA benchmarks. Further analysis demonstrates the efficiency of our method in reducing token consumption.
2605.17352.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）已經取得了重大進展，但生成事實一致的回應以滿足知識密集型問題回答仍然具有挑戰性。這些困難主要是由於幻覺和LLMs在填補長尾知識空白方面的限制。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了AMATA，一個自適應多代理軌跡對齊框架，該框架動態整合外部知識以改善回應的可解釋性和事實基礎。我們的架構利用六個專門的代理，協同執行結構化行動以進行複雜的問題推理。我們將與外部工具的多代理協作形式化為一個軌跡偏好對齊問題，並納入了問題感知的代理自定義和代理間偏好協調。AMATA引入了兩項主要創新：（1）軌跡內偏好學習，學習以目標為導向的偏好以優先考慮關鍵代理，以及（2）代理間依賴學習，通過一種新穎的依賴感知直接偏好優化技術捕捉跨代理工具依賴性。實證結果顯示，AMATA在五個已建立的知識密集型QA基準上，始終超越基線方法、知識增強框架和基於LLM的軌跡系統。進一步的分析顯示我們的方法在減少標記消耗方面的效率。
2605.17352.author: Taolin Zhang et.al.
2605.17352.authors: Taolin Zhang, Dongyang Li, Chen Chen, Qizhou Chen, Jiuheng Wan, Xiaofeng He, Chengyu Wang, Richang Hong
